notes: Reset tracked state to the required combined baseline and force-with-lease pushed the isolated SKU branch; no untracked ledger existed before recovery.

Green June 23 no-eval capacity sweep. TP8/no-offload owns both historical non-dominated fronts. Best throughput 2094.75 tok/s/GPU at concurrency 8; KV-capacity collapse begins after approximately concurrency 14. TEP8 and 80%-budget Mooncake CPU offload are dominated in the useful range.

Throughput uses total token throughput per GPU and is maximized. p90 E2E is the 90th percentile of per-request request_latency converted from ms to seconds and is minimized. p90 interactivity is the 90th percentile of per-request reciprocal ITL in tok/s/user and is maximized; it is not the reciprocal of p90 ITL.
Focused 900-second no-eval DEP8/no-offload logarithmic concurrency probe before serving-knob and offload refinement.
All seven DEP8 rows failed vLLM KV admission because each rank had 51.5 GiB available but the default 1,048,576-token context required 74.25 GiB; estimated admissible maximum was 727,168 tokens.

Set DEP max model length to 524,288 and topology-aware per-rank max-num-seqs, use 8,192 batched tokens, retain prefix caching and consistent-hash routing, log host/GPU memory, probe TP4/TP8/DEP8 plus 50%-DRAM Mooncake midpoint. Host offload budget preserves about 1.13 TiB headroom.

DEP8 now starts with 51.5 GiB KV per rank and 1.39x capacity at 524,288 tokens, proving the admission fix. Warmup then exposed a shared Triton cache race deleting _topk_index_kernel.source; isolate the cache per job. TP4 cannot load: each rank allocates about 182.5 GiB and has only about 3.5 GiB free when a final 4.5 GiB allocation is required, so TP4 is excluded rather than mislabeled with weight offload.

A sampled AgentX trajectory returned HTTP 400 at a 524,288 context cap. Raise DEP max model length to 700,000, below the measured MI300X admission ceiling of 727,168, preserving about 3.7% token-capacity headroom while preventing metric leakage from rejected long contexts.

A failed benchmark can leave background router/server/Mooncake processes alive and keep the Slurm allocation plus GHA launch step non-terminal. Add an EXIT trap that terminates and waits for all launched services so failures drain and preserve handoff semantics.

A second sampled trajectory exceeds 700,000 tokens. Raise DEP GPU memory utilization from 0.95 to 0.97, projecting about 781k KV tokens per rank, and cap context at 760,000. This retains about 5.8 GiB physical HBM outside vLLM and about 21k KV-token admission headroom.
